title: Burning down a corpo VPN: weaponizing OpenVPN config injection
excerpt: A misconfigured ovpn distribution endpoint let me inject directives that run as root on every employee laptop. Here's how it broke, and how to stop bleeding.

title: On staying analog: why I still take handwritten notes
excerpt: Every productivity app I've tried wants to be a database. I don't need a database. I need a place to think slowly.

title: Reversing a 'secure' chat app firmware in four hours
excerpt: Marketed as end-to-end encrypted. Was, technically. The key derivation was deterministic from the device serial.

title: The week I lived in tmux
excerpt: An experiment: no GUI apps for seven days. Just a terminal, a window manager, and stubbornness. Here's what broke.

title: DNS rebinding is back, and it's wearing a hoodie
excerpt: Local-network IoT dashboards keep reinventing the same mistake. A short tour of why your smart fridge is one malicious ad away from a botnet.

title: Three years since I quit the corp job
excerpt: What I thought freelance security work would be, versus what it actually is. Mostly: more invoicing, less Mr. Robot.
